# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import * as jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';

const SECRET_KEY = process.env.SECRET_KEY || 'dev-secret'; // Replace with your actual secret key in prod

function verifyJwt(req, res, next) {
  const token = req.headers['x-access-token'] as string;

  if (token) {
    try {
      const decoded = jwt.decode(token, { complete: true }) as any;
      const user = {
        _id: decoded.payload._id,
        email: decoded.payload.email
      };
      
      // Store the user in a session store or in local storage for simplicity
      req.session.user = user;

      next();
    } catch (e) {
      console.error('Error verifying JWT:', e);
      res.status(401).send({ message: 'Invalid token' });
    }
  } else {
    res.status(401).send({ message: 'No token provided' });
  }
}

export default verifyJwt;
